name: T L C School Bus Co
districts_or_customers: School District of Philadelphia (per litigation history)
recent_news: TLC School Bus Company LLC filed Chapter bankruptcy in 2015 (Case 2:15-bk-18042, U.S. Bankruptcy Court Eastern District of PA); related litigation involving Advance Business Capital LLC and the School District of Philadelphia suggests financial distress. Likely defunct or dormant. The active 'TLC Transportation' (Mercersburg, PA) is a separate, unrelated family-owned operator.
fit_notes: Not a viable tuck-in target. Entity filed bankruptcy in 2015 and shows no signs of an active operating school-bus contractor at the Plymouth Meeting address. Skip.

name: Bus Driver LLC
recent_news: No public web presence, no website, and no news located. Richard Finkelstein appears in FL business directory listings linked to Hiatus Road area entities, but no specific operating profile, fleet info, district contracts, or recent activity for 'Bus Driver LLC' was found. Cannot confirm it is an active independent school-bus operator vs. a holding/broker entity.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~9 employees, ~$410K revenue) with no verifiable web footprint. Not a meaningful tuck-in unless local Broward route concentration can be validated via Sunbiz/FLDOE; deprioritize.

name: L M School Bus Svc Inc
recent_news: No website, no news, no LinkedIn footprint located. Owner-managed micro-operator (Rojo L. Suarez) at 820 NW 91st Ave, Pembroke Pines. Likely independent owner-operator school-bus contractor; cannot confirm scale, fleet, or district contracts (Broward County Public Schools area).
fit_notes: Likely independent owner-operator at sub-scale (~9 employees). Too small for standalone tuck-in but could be route-bundled with other Broward County micro-contractors if Summit pursues a roll-up of small FL operators. Deprioritize.

name: Magic School Bus
recent_news: No website or active web presence located for this Hollywood, FL entity. Note: 'The Magic Bus Transport Corp' in The Hammocks/Miami (est. 2016) is a separate operator. 'Magic School Bus' is also a Disney/Scholastic trademarked entertainment property, which complicates branding/visibility. Project Magic School Bus (Broward County Schools program) is unrelated.
fit_notes: Sub-scale owner-operator with no verifiable web footprint. Trademark-conflicted name suggests informal/family operation. Not a priority tuck-in; would require Sunbiz/route-permit validation.

name: Nelson's Bus Svc
principals: Dave Nelson (3rd gen, Director of Service)
recent_news: Independent, third-generation family business founded 1940 by Wendell & Dorothy Nelson. Two related operations: (1) Nelson's Bus Services Inc. — Platinum Certified Thomas Built Buses dealer for WI, N. IL, Upper MI (HQ Whitewater, WI; expanded via 1997 purchase of Northland Equipment's Thomas dealership); (2) 'Ride With Nelson's' — school-bus contractor transporting >4,000 students/day across McFarland, Monona Grove, and Whitewater districts. Stoughton address is one of the operating depots. Not on Beacon Mobility/First Student portfolios as of 2026.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: 85+ year independent family-owned multi-district WI contractor (>4,000 daily students across 3 districts) plus a valuable Thomas Built dealer/parts/service franchise across WI/N. IL/Upper MI — rare combined platform. 3rd-gen leadership signals medium succession optionality (no 4th-gen public profile). Wisconsin is non-Summit but a logical Midwest expansion footprint; the bundled contractor + dealer + parts ops would also create captive maintenance synergies for Summit's regional fleet. Recommend outreach.
